hero.question: Anduril IPO before 2027?
hero.prob_take: Anduril's IPO odds just cratered 22 points, signaling serious doubt that the defense AI company clears a public exit in the next 3 years, likely from weaker defense spending forecasts and the reality that most defense contractors take forever to IPO. At 29%, the market is probably overcounting how badly recent geopolitical noise spooked institutional demand, when Anduril's actual growth trajectory and defense sector tailwinds remain intact for a 2026 window.

daily_take.headline: Anduril's IPO Window Just Slammed Shut. Smart Money Already Left.
daily_take.deck: Anduril dropped 22 points in 24 hours to 29%, signaling a real shift in founder confidence or funding trajectory. The defense AI startup was riding hype into late 2024, but this crater suggests either a delay into 2027 or internal signals the market missed. Short this aggressively if you see any more founder commentary about "taking time to build." The dumb money bought the narrative. The smart money is exiting first.

sidebar.headline: Somalia strikes market is pricing certainty. 97.2% odds mean you're getting paid peanuts if the US hits 10-13 targets. Skip this one unless you think the range is actually tighter.
sidebar.label: Somalia Strikes (Feb 2026): 97.2%

sidebar.headline: India at 44.5% on T20 2026 is a classic squares vs sharps split. Their recent form is soft but home-field advantage in tournament year still underpriced relative to recent betting patterns.
sidebar.label: India T20 World Cup 2026: 44.5%

sidebar.headline: Applied Intuition dropping 11 points suggests the IPO window is tightening across the sector. If Anduril is struggling, a B2B self-driving stack company won't run faster. Look for 18.5% to keep sagging.
sidebar.label: Applied Intuition IPO: 18.5%
